title: Podman running unprivileged Buildah container does not work.
body:
**Description**

<!--
Buildah has somehow regressed on being able to run as a container with un privileged mode.

-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. First build the Buildah container:
```
# containerid=$(buildah from scratch)
# scratchmnt=$(buildah mount $containerid)
# dnf install --installroot $scratchmnt --release 27 buildah --setopt install_weak_deps=false -y
# dnf clean all --installroot $scratchmnt --release 27
# buildah config --cmd "\-\-help" $containerid
# buildah config --entrypoint /usr/bin/buildah $containerid
# buildah config --author "wgh @ redhat.com" --created-by "ipbabble" --label name=buildah $containerid
```
2. Commit the container:
`# buildah commit $containerid buildah`

3. The try and run Podman running Buildah container with a Dockerfile

A) First try it using the containers own /var/lib/contiainer directory. In this case you can't use overlayfs on overlayfs and so you need to tell Buildah to use vfs as the storage driver
```
podman --log-level debug run --network host  -v /home/whenry/dfs:/tmp:Z up-buildah --storage-driver vfs bud -f /tmp/Dockerfile
```
or 
B) When this didn't work I decided to mount in a directory called /tmp/containers as /var/lib/containers and could drop --storage-driver vfs becuase /tmp is not overlayfs (right?)
```
podman --log-level debug run --network host  -v /tmp/containers:/var/lib/containers:Z -v /home/whenry/dfs:/tmp:Z up-buildah --storage-driver vfs bud -f /tmp/Dockerfile
```

**Describe the results you received:**
If you leave --log-level out then the command just returns after doing nothing.

When you run with --log-level debug then:
A)
```
# podman --log-level debug run --network host  -v /tmp/containers:/var/lib/containers:Z -v /home/whenry/dfs:/tmp:Z up-buildah bud -f /tmp/Dockerfile

buildah version 0.15 (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)

```

```

```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fedora 27 on a laptop not in a VM.

comments.body: If you run your container through docker does it work?

The container appears to have been created successfully and stopped on its own - the lack of output doesn't strike me as a podman bug as such.

Can you get a `podman ps -a` or similar after the container exited to get the exit code?

Also, can you try running buildah in the container with --debug specified? I suspect this is an error doing storage init (probably need more caps than we give by default for VFS, might also be seccomp blocking mount-related calls?)

The overlay storage driver mounts overlay filesystems, and unless I'm mistaken, that's not allowed inside of an unprivileged container, so it can't be used.  Running buildah with its --debug flag should help to verify that it's being started, and what's happening after that.
This is being worked at on Buildah so I am closing the issue here.
